description:
The sudo package can be installed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf install sudo
rationale:
<tt>sudo</tt> is a program designed to allow a system administrator to give
limited root privileges to users and log root activity. The basic philosophy
is to give as few privileges as possible but still allow system users to
get their work done.
ocil_clause: the sudo package is not installed
ocil:
Run the following command to determine if the sudo package is installed: 
$ rpm -q sudo
fixtext:
The sudo package can be installed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf install sudo
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must have the sudo package installed.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must have the sudo package installed.
stig.vuldiscussion: "sudo" is a program designed to allow a system administrator to give limited root privileges to users and log root activity. The basic philosophy is to give as few privileges as possible but still allow system users to get their work done.
stig.checktext:
Verify that Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 sudo package is installed with the following command:

$ dnf list --installed sudo

Example output:

sudo.x86_64          1.9.5p2-7.el9

If the "sudo" package is not installed, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
The  sudo  package can be installed with the following command:

$ sudo dnf install sudo

title: Install sudo Package
